notes: Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in June 1998. Alpha architecture discontinued 2001-2004. HPTC group dissolved.
Major HPTC competitor. SGI filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2006; re-emerged but filed Chapter 7 in 2009; assets acquired by Rackable Systems (renamed SGI). Later acquired by HPE in 2016.
HPTC competitor cited; RS/6000 SP MPP architecture criticized by Aberdeen. IBM Power remained major HPC platform; IBM Summit (2018) became world's fastest supercomputer.

successor: HP acquired Compaq 2002; HPE spun out 2015
notes: HP acquired Convex Computer in 1995. Aberdeen noted HP not investing meaningfully in Convex HPTC. HP merged with Compaq 2002; HPE spun off 2015.
New HPTC entrant via Floating Point Systems (Cray) and Thinking Machines acquisitions. Aberdeen questioned long-term HPTC commitment. Sun acquired by Oracle in 2010.

successor: Acquired by Digital; Memory Channel technology contributed
notes: Contributed Memory Channel technology to Digital's TruCluster. Encore acquired by Digital; Memory Channel became a key DEC clustering differentiator.
Publisher of Pro/ENGINEER (MCAD software); Pro-E Bench97 benchmark used in study. PTC remains active enterprise software vendor.
Traditional supercomputer vendor referenced implicitly. SGI acquired Cray Research in 1996; Cray Inc. spun out 2000; acquired by HPE 2019.
